html:
Erik Magnusson can refer to several different people:

  • Erik Magnusson of Norway (1268–1299), King of Norway from 1280–1299
  • Erik Magnusson (c. 1282–1318), Swedish Duke, who died at Nyköpingshus after the Nyköping Banquet
  • Erik Magnusson of Sweden (1339–1359), King of Sweden from 1356–1359
  • Eric Magnusson (1897–1973), Swedish actor and singer
  • Erik Magnusson (1900–1997), Swedish politician
  • Erik Magnusson (1919–2012), Swedish visual artist
  • Erik Magnusson (1930–2001), Danish-Swedish architect
  • Erik Magnussen (1940–2014), Danish designer
  • Erik Magnusson (born 1942), Swedish Social Democratic politician
  • Erik Magnusson (born 1979), Swedish actor
  • Erik Magnusson (journalist), winner of the Guldspaden award

How certain are we about this information?

For people from the medieval period, there are often contemporary written sources (letters, chronicles, church documents), so the main facts are usually well-documented. However, these sources were mostly written by the Church and those in power, and they are unevenly preserved, which can distort and limit our understanding.
tldr: The name Erik Magnusson has been used by many different people throughout history, including kings, actors, and politicians.
lattlast:
Because the name is so common, there are many well-known individuals named Erik Magnusson. These include medieval kings from Norway and Sweden, as well as modern people who have worked in fields such as art, theater, and politics.

When reading about historical figures from the Middle Ages, one can often rely on major events because old letters and documents exist. However, since these texts were written by the church or powerful individuals, the information is sometimes limited to their perspectives.

For modern individuals like actors, architects, and journalists, the information is more readily available. The text demonstrates that the name appears in various professions and countries, making it important to know which specific person is being referred to.
